name: Helium
summary: CRITICAL CASCADE — COUNTDOWN 5-7 DAYS: Helium inventories approaching exhaustion at full production rates (early April). CRITICAL CORRECTION: Samsung's '6-month buffer' = strategic reserves, NOT working fab inventory (~1 week). 200 helium containers stranded near Hormuz. KOSPI -4.26% to 5,052 (near bear market, -19.1% monthly, worst major index globally). DRAM/HBM +80-90% Q/Q. 'RAMmageddon.' TurboQuant (Google) compounds by questioning HBM demand (~40% of KOSPI decline). No confirmed halt yet but 5-7 day window.

signals.content: Iran war threatening supply of helium — 30% of world supply from Qatar's Ras Laffan, now offline. One of only two plants producing semiconductor-grade helium. No viable substitute exists.

Analysts project minimum 2-3 month helium production shutdown, 4-6 months before supply chain returns. HP, Dell, Lenovo, Acer, ASUS warned enterprise clients of 15-20% price hikes. TSMC said no significant impact yet but monitoring.

TrendForce published analysis naming South Korea as hardest-hit — Samsung and SK Hynix sourced 64.7% of helium from Qatar. SK Hynix on a 'two-week clock' to diversify or face production cuts at full production rates (Tom's Hardware). Korean chipmakers claim 6-month stockpiles at rationed consumption but 2 weeks at full rates. Helium spot prices surged 40% in a single week, analysts warning of $2,000/MCF (up from ~$500 pre-conflict). Samsung's HeRS (Helium Reuse System) operational on select lines reducing consumption by ~4.7 tons/year per line, potential 18.6% total reduction if deployed company-wide — meaningful but insufficient. TSMC more diversified (30% Qatar, 30% US, 40% other) giving more buffer than Korean fabs. Phil Kornbluth estimated 'minimum' 2-3 month production shutdown and 4-6 month normalization.

CLOCK UPDATE: TrendForce's March 24 'two-week clock' assessment means approximately 8 days remain before Asian chip fab helium inventories exhaust at full production rates (early April). KOSPI -10.6% monthly, Samsung -8.2% from peak. No new alternative supply sources have been confirmed. The cascade path (war → Qatar → helium → semiconductors → KOSPI/SMH) is the most validated multi-hop cascade in the graph with 3 independent confirmations (TrendForce, Nikkei Asia, Korea International Trade Association). Container freight Shanghai-Dubai doubled to $4,000+/FEU reflecting Gulf shipping disruption. (Sources: TrendForce timeline, yfinance KOSPI data, Freightos FBX)

DATA RECONCILIATION: Samsung reportedly maintains 'roughly six months' of helium stock (Korea Plus/Carra Globe), contradicting TrendForce's 'two-week clock' (March 24). Both can be true: Samsung = large buyer with strategic reserves + HeRS recycling. Industry average and SK hynix = shorter runway. Samsung also exploring Russian helium supply. The 6-day countdown applies to INDUSTRY (especially SK hynix), not Samsung specifically. (Sources: Korea Plus, Carra Globe, TrendForce, Tom's Hardware)

KOSPI closed at 5,052.46, down 4.26% — significantly worse than morning's intraday reading. Approaching bear market territory (-19.1% from late-Feb peak). Samsung -5.2%, SK hynix -5.2%. Gap narrowed — both hit equally, weakening Samsung HeRS differentiation thesis. Foreign investors sold 35 trillion won in March (all-time monthly record). CRITICAL CORRECTION: Samsung's '6-month buffer' is misleading — working fab inventory is approximately ONE WEEK. Six months = supply-chain strategic reserves. Production depends on continuous inbound shipments. Additionally, 200 specialized helium containers stranded near Hormuz — even available helium is physically stuck. Rerouting via Cape of Good Hope adds 10-14 days AND boil-off reduces usable volume. Memory prices: DRAM/HBM +80-90% Q/Q, server-grade spot +400%. Cloud providers raising prices 30-40%. Silicon Motion CEO: 'never happened before' — all memory types in severe shortage. Wikipedia now has '2024-present global memory supply shortage' article — nicknamed 'RAMmageddon.' TurboQuant (Google's AI memory compression algorithm, March 25) compounds crisis by questioning HBM demand at these prices — ~40% of KOSPI decline attributable to non-crisis TurboQuant factor. No confirmed production halt yet — window now 5-7 days (early April).

edges.context: Qatar's Ras Laffan = 30% of world helium. One of only 2 semiconductor-grade helium plants globally. Now offline.

Helium shortage → semiconductor manufacturing disruption → AI infrastructure uncertainty → tech valuation pressure. TrendForce confirmed country-level exposure data. Cascade validated by KOSPI 6.5% crash.

Qatar supplies 64.7% of South Korea's helium (Korea International Trade Association). Samsung and SK hynix fab operations directly threatened. KOSPI -4.26% to 5,052, -19.1% monthly, near bear market. Triple cascade validated: helium→semiconductor→KOSPI by 3 independent sources (TrendForce, Nikkei Asia, KITA).

trigger_points.condition: Helium shortage confirmed affecting TSMC/Samsung production lines
trigger_points.mechanism: Would directly impact chip output, AI training capacity, and tech earnings. SK Hynix 2-week clock at full production rates is the nearest trigger.
